summary: Added a reduced-motion-aware pulse trail and stage spark layer to the Path Map Chapter Questline so the chapter run-map feels alive while preserving local-only Event Proof focus behavior.

queries: dashboard microinteractions subtle animation progress indicator UX performance
game UI quest progress animation subtle pulse line dashboard UX
web animation prefers-reduced-motion dashboard microinteractions best practices
pattern_notes: Use motion as a small contextual status signal instead of decorative noise.
Progress indicators and subtle pulses help users see that a system or run is active.
Respect prefers-reduced-motion so non-essential motion can be minimized safely.

ui_contract: Render a path-chapter-quest-pulse inside the Questline meter.
Render one path-chapter-quest-spark inside each stage without affecting accessible text.
Animate the pulse across the existing progress meter using --questline-progress.
Animate subtle stage sparks for unlocked, advancing, and gated stages.
Disable visible pulse/spark effects under prefers-reduced-motion.
Keep Event Proof stage focus and generated texture rendering unchanged.

red_green.red: pytest tests\test_agent_company_atlas_path_chapter_questline.py -q failed before pulse wiring on missing path-chapter-quest-pulse markup.
red_green.green: pytest tests\test_agent_company_atlas_path_chapter_questline.py -q passed after pulse/spark markup, styles, reduced-motion handling, docs, and cache token wiring.

browser_green_route.evidence: Rendered one Chapter Questline with one pulse, six sparks, six stages, three clickable source stages, generated texture path-chapter-questline-20260618.png, pulse animation questlinePulseTrail, spark animation questlineSparkRise, no horizontal overflow, zero console errors, and spawn-stage click focused the matching trace in the local Event Proof grid.

reduced_motion.evidence: Static CSS and browser-loaded stylesheet include prefers-reduced-motion handling for path-chapter-quest-pulse and path-chapter-quest-spark while preserving loaded keyframes.

responsive.evidence: Browser sweep at 390x820, 820x900, 1280x900, and 1600x1000 kept one pulse, six sparks, six stages, questlinePulseTrail/questlineSparkRise animations, generated texture, zero horizontal overflow, and zero console errors.
